tool.name: Interactive overlap heatmap

param.name: input1
param.label: Input file:
param.help: Enter the matrix with the counts of common elements

param.name: input2
param.label: Input file:
param.help: Enter the matrix with the counts of common elements names/ids

param.name: threshold
param.label: Count threshold
param.help: Threshold indicating which counts filter. If an element have a number of common objects that is less than the threshold between all other elements, it will be removed from the final matrix

param.name: x_label
param.label: x axis label:
param.help: Label to be used for x axis in the plot

param.name: y_label
param.label: y axis label:
param.help: Label to be used for y axis in the plot

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool generates an interactive heatmap for visualizing pairwise overlap analyses. The input consists of: - a count matrix containing the number of shared elements between pairs of entities; - a name matrix containing the corresponding shared elements.
Each heatmap cell represents the overlap between two entities. In addition to displaying overlap counts, hovering over a cell reveals the underlying shared elements associated with that overlap. This allows users to inspect the data behind the visualization without manually cross-referencing the input matrices.
The diagonal is automatically set to NaN to improve visualization of pairwise overlaps. An optional threshold can be applied to remove entities whose overlap counts remain below the specified threshold for all comparisons. Custom labels for the x and y axes can also be provided.
**Typical use cases**
This tool can be used to explore overlaps between genes, phenotypes, diseases, samples, pathways, or any other entities represented by pairwise overlap matrices. The resulting interactive heatmap can be used for exploratory data analysis, similarity assessment, and identification of highly related entities.
